items.title: SA Police return to Yunta as part of Gus Lamont investigation

children.content: Police have returned to Yunta in South Australia’s far north as part of their investigation into the disappearance of four-year-old boy Gus Lamont.

items.title: Late pastoralist's family loses appeal over judgement that father was responsible for bushfire

children.content: A court judgement that found pastoralist Tom Brinkworth liable for a 2021 bushfire, five months after he died, has been upheld after an appeal by Mr Brinkworth's family.

image.altText: Smoke about the Lucindale grass fire

items.title: Serious crash involving road train closes Stuart Highway in far north SA

children.content: A major outback highway in South Australia's north is closed in both directions after a crash involving a road train and a vehicle early this morning.

image.altText: Road signage on the side of a highway intersection in the Northern Territory.

items.title: 'A devastating mess': Owner laments destruction and theft of organ pipes at former church

children.content: The pipe theft comes days after a bronze statue was stolen from the centre of a fountain in Adelaide's inner north-eastern suburbs.

image.altText: A man standing in a darkened church with organ keys in the foreground

items.title: Factory workers reunite 16 years after closure of major food manufacturer

children.content: Sixteen years after workers clocked off for the final time, Berrivale workers reunited to remember the cannery and preserving factory that employed generations of locals.

image.altText: About a dozen women working on a production line in a factory.

items.title: Cricket's next generation of pace knows fast bowling can take a toll

children.content: Cricket has few more glorious sights than a fast bowler terrorising a top-class batting order, but becoming a speedster is not without its pains, as the nation's next generation is finding out.

gallery.altText: Simrth Gill tosses up a cricket ball, while holding a cricket bat.

thumbnail.altText: Simrth Gill tosses up a cricket ball, while holding a cricket bat.

items.title: Travis Head and wife Jess cop social media abuse after IPL on-field incident

children.content: Australian cricketer Travis Head and his wife Jess Head have their Instagram accounts blasted with vile comments after a series of tense on-field exchanges between Head and Indian star Virat Kohli in an Indian Premier League match.

image.altText: Australian batsman Travis head looks at his bat while Indian fielder Virat Kohli smiles and shouts.

items.title: 'We're taking him home': Aboriginal stockman's remains repatriated

children.content: An Aboriginal stockman whose remains have been held by the South Australian Museum for six decades will this week be returned to Country in a remote national park.

image.altText: The start of a repatriation process at the SA Museum.

items.title: Truro businesses call for certainty on freight bypass project

children.content: SA businesses situated along a major thoroughfare say they are holding back on making decisions about their future, amid uncertainty surrounding a slated road project that could divert traffic around the area.

image.altText: an image of a car's driver side mirror with a truck reflected, driving through a town

items.title: Dog-friendly cafe issued warning after customer complaint

children.content: Kellie Hunter opened her cafe in the Adelaide Hills in 2023 as a friendly place for dogs and their owners, but the local council has now put a stop to it after one complaint.

image.altText: A woman speaking to a dog standing on its back legs at a cafe serving window

items.title: Amid push to rename Grey, SA's largest federal seat, who was its namesake?

children.content: Sir George Grey was the former governor of South Australia and is remembered as a "bundle of contradictions" in both Australia and New Zealand, where he played a key role in the Māori wars. But amid calls for the seat that bears his name to be renamed, who was Grey?

image.altText: A collage showing a historic portrait photo in black and white with a map of the seat of Grey and a painting from the NZ wars

items.title: Government bails out Rex's multi-million-dollar debt to regional airports

children.content: Council-owned airports have been bailed out by the federal government to the tune of $4.8 million, $2 million of which was for NSW alone.

image.altText: The outside of an aircraft printed with the letters REX in blue and orange

items.title: Iron ore processing project dumped by BHP for not being profitable enough

children.content: Internal BHP documents reveal how the mining giant planned to set up an iron ore processing facility in the Pilbara to cut carbon emissions before backing away from it entirely.

gallery.altText: A photo of an iron ore mine is ripped down the middle and replaced by a photo of a BHP building.

thumbnail.altText: A photo of an iron ore mine is ripped down the middle and replaced by a photo of a BHP building.

items.title: Angus Taylor pressures PM to detail small business CGT carve-outs in QT

children.content: The opposition leader has pressured the prime minister to reveal details of small business carve-outs for capital gains tax discount changes. Follow live.

image.altText: Angus Taylor looks at his backbench in the lower house.

items.title: Santos boss 'can't believe' gas reservation would force loss-making prices

children.content: The federal government's draft framework would require gas producers to supply 20 per cent of their exports to the domestic market, with limited exceptions, which gas producers warn would "undermine" future supply.

image.altText: Kevin Gallagher wears a suit at a press conference in Darwin.

items.title: K-pop triumphs at American Music Awards as Pussycat Dolls lead noughties nostalgia

children.content: Taylor Swift led the night with eight nominations but didn't take home a single award, while 80s rock star Billy Idol was honoured with a Lifetime Achievement Award.

gallery.altText: Three women in red latex pose on stage.
Billy Idol in black leather and a purple shirt poses.

thumbnail.altText: Three women in red latex pose on stage.
Billy Idol in black leather and a purple shirt poses.

items.title: Hypersonic missile attack a sign of Putin's 'bruised ego', expert says

children.content: Russia escalates attacks on Kyiv, using a hypersonic missile in one of its largest assaults on the Ukrainian capital since the start of the four-year war, and warns foreigners to leave the city ahead of further strikes.

image.altText: A man with a bandaged head with blood on his t-shirt pats his dog while sheltering in an underground bomb shelter.

items.title: 'ISIS bride' under exclusion order not returning to Australia, ABC confirms

children.content: The majority of the final group of Australians living in a notorious Syrian camp for the families of killed and captured Islamic State militants are expected to arrive in Australia soon. Here's what we know about them.

image.altText: A group of three women wearing burqas walk along a camp in Syria.

items.title: Fan in Australian jersey catches Aussie-hit homer in 15-year first

children.content: Curtis Mead hit two home runs for Washington against fellow Australian Travis Bazzana's Cleveland side as two Australian-born players faced off in an MLB game for the first time since 2017. But it was a man in an Australian supporters shirt who stole the show.

gallery.altText: Baseball fan Jack Seiple sits in an Australia jersey

thumbnail.altText: Baseball fan Jack Seiple sits in an Australia jersey

items.title: Israeli politician calls for investigation after Gaza flotilla activists allege abuse

children.content: One of Israel's most prominent opposition politicians has called for an investigation into the alleged abuse of activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla, which was intercepted by the Israeli military in the Mediterranean last week.

image.altText: Yair Lapid speaks at a lectern gesturing with one hand.

items.title: One woman will make Japanese history by going on maternity leave

children.content: A Japanese mayor will take maternity leave this year, becoming the first in the country's history to do so while in office.

image.altText: A woman in a grey suit smiles at the camera and holds her fist up in a soccer stadium

items.title: The many phases of Miles Davis, who would have turned 100 today

children.content: From Illinois to New York to Australia, legendary trumpeter Miles Davis helped shape the direction of jazz with albums like Birth of the Cool and Kind of Blue.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of Miles Davis playing the trumpet. His eyes look down and he has a serious expression.
Miles Davis plays his red trumpet on-stage; he's bathed in orange light

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of Miles Davis playing the trumpet. His eyes look down and he has a serious expression.
Miles Davis plays his red trumpet on-stage; he's bathed in orange light

items.title: Using AI to manage your budget? This is what experts want you to know

children.content: "Hallucinations", privacy and other factors to consider if you're turning to artificial intelligence for financial advice.

image.altText: The hands hands of a woman typing, while she's reclined on the sofa.

items.title: I'm making a conscious effort to break free from unspoken family trauma

children.content: Motherhood has a way of making family patterns impossible to ignore. Suddenly you start asking questions you never asked before.

image.altText: Carlie Schofield pictures as a baby, being held by her mum.

avatar.alt: Carlie Schofield wears and maroon top smiling in a room with an Aboriginal artwork on the wall.

items.title: 'Catastrophic failure' after drones malfunction at Vivid Sydney show

children.content: The incident occurred not long into Monday's 7.30pm "star-bound" show when almost 90 drones began falling out of formation and into the harbour around Cockle Bay.

image.altText: Drones carrying colourful lights form an unidentifiable shape over a bay at night

gallery.altText: Drone lights falling into the water below, a skyscraper in the background.

thumbnail.altText: Drone lights falling into the water below, a skyscraper in the background.

items.title: Power price drops for most customers as batteries flatten peak

children.content: Surging levels of renewable energy and better reliability from coal-fired generators are set to give consumers a break, with benchmark power prices to fall up to 10 per cent for consumers and more for small businesses.

image.altText: rows of battery containers at sunset

items.title: Australian stocks fall as US strikes dampen investor sentiment

children.content: The local share market is in the red, while shares in stock exchange operator ASX Ltd have tumbled on a trading update. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Teary council officer tells ICAC they 'should be ashamed of yourselves'

children.content: Roxanne Thornton accuses Independent Commission Against Corruption of "laying the boot in" after four days of questioning over the manipulation of the recruitment process at the council to secure jobs for friends and associates.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair in maroon and navy top holding a tissue

items.title: Bringing Them Home inaction a 'woeful failure', ex-commissioner says

children.content: Mick Dodson heard "soul-destroying" testimony from more than 500 First Nations people during a landmark inquiry. He says that 29 years later, it's time for action.

image.altText: A man, wearing a tshirt and an akubra hat, stands outside in front of trees.

items.title: Spirit ferries operator set for major restructure under Tasmanian Labor plan

children.content: The two government businesses responsible for the botched Spirit of Tasmania ship replacement program would undergo a major restructure, with one stripped of all assets, under a Tasmanian Labor government.

image.altText: A large white and red ship docked in Hobart.

items.title: What do plummeting clearance rates mean for the property market?

children.content: Early signs of an Australian housing market correction are now clearly visible, and at the heart of the recent property market weakness is less vendor power.

image.altText: An overhead shot of a housing estate.

items.title: Can State of Origin save an NRL season that's losing its way?

children.content: Players, coaches and fans are disillusioned with the rule changes that have come to define this NRL season but ahead of Origin I can the interstate series put a broken season back together?

image.altText: A man is tackled during a State of Origin match

items.title: 170m chimneys come crashing down as power station demolition begins

children.content: The demolition of the Liddell Power Station, which powered homes and businesses in NSW for more than 50 years, is underway.

image.altText: Two chimneys crash together in a controlled demolition creating smoke and dust.
Power Station being demolished.

items.title: Brad Scott sacked as Essendon coach after one win in past 24 matches

children.content: Essendon sacks head coach Brad Scott as the Bombers languish at the bottom of the AFL ladder.

image.altText: AFL coach looking annoyed as he walks off the ground

items.title: One world record and a whole lot of regret for Enhanced Games athletes

children.content: Despite the drugs and the super suits, the Enhanced Games delivered little more than a smattering of PBs and a single world fastest time for the hefty price of an athlete's hard-earned credibility.

image.altText: Kristian Gkolomeev,holds up two trophies
